items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Wemby on his increased activity inside in Game 3:

"The goal is always to go inside. The best shot in the game is an alley-oop.  The most efficient shot.  But teams don't just let you do that."

items.title: Watching Sergio De Larrea, a potential option for the Celtics with the 27th pick.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Watching Sergio De Larrea, a potential option for the Celtics with the 27th pick.

He has a good feel for the game and keeps his head up when he's dribbling.

Here, he sees former NBA center Usman Garuba isn't back, and he attacks downhill for a two-handed dunk in transition.

items.title: NYC native Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
NYC native Julian Champagnie on the atmosphere at Madison Square Garden:

"The initial reaction of just running out there, seeing all the celebrities, the fans, how loud it was, the music, it was definitely a surreal feeling that I'll carry for life. ... It was pretty cool."

items.title: Spurs focused solely on film study Tuesday.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs focused solely on film study Tuesday.

"These are very physical games," Stephon Castle said. "Both teams are getting beat up. We're at the end of the year, so everybody is tired at this point of the year...Everybody needs to rest."

items.title: Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Keldon Johnson scored just 7 points, but Game 3 was one of his best of the playoffs. In 17 minutes, he was a team-best plus-14 and did a good job muscling up against Towns.

"I just tried to make it tough on him, make his catches tough, try to make him uncomfortable," he said.

items.title: June 9, 1995: Houston's Hakeem Olajuwon hits a fadeaway jumper during a 117-106 win at Orlando in Game 2 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 9, 1995: Houston's Hakeem Olajuwon hits a fadeaway jumper during a 117-106 win at Orlando in Game 2 of the NBA Finals.

Olajuwon had 34 PTS/11 REB/4 BLK and Clyde Drexler added 23 PTS for the Rockets, who took a 2-0 series lead. Shaquille O'Neal had 33 PTS/12 REB/7 AST for the Magic.

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Brian Taylor. Princeton SG went to play w/Dr J Nets in ABA + was ROY & 2x all-star. NJ let him go in 76 + he was good w/KC, DEN, SDC thru 82. Led ABA + NBA in 3%. Retired in 82 due to Achilles tear. Underrated & would've been analytics fave.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Brian Taylor. Princeton SG went to play w/Dr J Nets in ABA + was ROY & 2x all-star. NJ let him go in 76 + he was good w/KC, DEN, SDC thru 82. Led ABA + NBA in 3%. Retired in 82 due to Achilles tear. Underrated & would've been analytics fave.

items.title: The passive voice regarding the description of action happening in public (or even in private but witnessed first hand) in journalism needed to go away a long, long time ago.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
The passive voice regarding the description of action happening in public (or even in private but witnessed first hand) in journalism needed to go away a long, long time ago. 
If you saw it, you saw it. 
If you saw someone do it, they did it.
